metadata.description: Challenges validating security scanning infrastructure: Detekt zero-issues policy, OWASP CVSS threshold, Gitleaks custom rules, path traversal prevention via normalizePath(), SBOM generation capability, and CodeQL configuration

challenges.name: Detekt Zero-Issues Policy Enforcement
challenges.description: Verify that the Detekt static analysis configuration enforces maxIssues: 0 across all rule sets. Run './gradlew detekt' and verify that the build fails on any new issue. Check detekt.yml configuration for maxIssues setting, verify that no rule sets are disabled, and confirm that the failOnSeverity threshold is set to 'warning' or lower so that warnings also break the build.

outputs.name: max_issues_setting
outputs.description: The maxIssues value configured in detekt.yml

outputs.name: disabled_rule_sets
outputs.description: Rule sets that are disabled in configuration

outputs.name: fail_on_severity
outputs.description: The failOnSeverity threshold configured

outputs.name: detekt_exit_code
outputs.description: Exit code from running ./gradlew detekt

outputs.name: total_issues_found
outputs.description: Number of issues found by Detekt

assertions.message: Detekt maxIssues must be set to 0 for zero-tolerance policy
No Detekt rule sets may be disabled
Detekt must report zero issues on the current codebase

challenges.name: OWASP Dependency Check CVSS 7.0 Threshold
challenges.description: Verify that the OWASP Dependency Check is configured with a CVSS threshold of 7.0 (High severity) so that any dependency with a known vulnerability scoring 7.0 or above causes the build to fail. Check the CI configuration and Gradle plugin settings for failBuildOnCVSS, verify the NVD data feed is configured, and confirm that suppression files only suppress false positives with documented justification.

outputs.name: cvss_threshold
outputs.description: The failBuildOnCVSS threshold value configured

outputs.name: nvd_feed_configured
outputs.description: Whether the NVD data feed URL is configured

outputs.name: suppression_file_exists
outputs.description: Whether a suppression file exists

outputs.name: suppressed_cves
outputs.description: List of suppressed CVEs with justifications

outputs.name: unjustified_suppressions
outputs.description: Suppressed CVEs without documented justification

assertions.message: OWASP failBuildOnCVSS must be set to 7.0 or lower (High severity threshold)
NVD data feed must be configured for OWASP Dependency Check
All CVE suppressions must have documented justification

challenges.name: Gitleaks Custom Rules Configuration (8 Rules)
challenges.description: Verify that the Gitleaks configuration includes all 8 custom rules for detecting secrets in the Yole codebase: (1) OAuth client secrets, (2) API keys for cloud storage providers, (3) SFTP/FTP passwords, (4) Firebase config tokens, (5) base64-encoded credentials, (6) JWT signing keys, (7) database connection strings with embedded passwords, (8) private key file contents. Run Gitleaks against test fixtures containing each secret type and verify all 8 are detected.

outputs.name: rules_configured
outputs.description: Number of custom rules in Gitleaks configuration

outputs.name: rules_detected
outputs.description: Map of rule ID to whether it detected its target secret in test fixtures

outputs.name: false_negatives
outputs.description: Rules that failed to detect their target secret

outputs.name: false_positives_in_tests
outputs.description: Legitimate test strings incorrectly flagged as secrets

assertions.message: At least 8 custom Gitleaks rules must be configured
All 8 Gitleaks rules must detect their target secret types
Every custom rule must successfully detect its target secret in test fixtures

challenges.name: Path Traversal Prevention via normalizePath() in All Services
challenges.description: Verify that normalizePath() from PathUtils.kt is called in all 8 protocol services for every file operation (listFiles, readFile, writeFile, deleteFile, getFileInfo, createDirectory). Statically verify that no file operation method accepts a raw path without normalization. Dynamically test with 20 traversal payloads including '../', '..\', URL-encoded variants, double-encoded variants, null-byte injection, and Unicode normalization attacks. All must resolve to safe paths within the service root.

outputs.name: static_analysis_results
outputs.description: Map of service to file operations verified to call normalizePath()

outputs.name: operations_without_normalization
outputs.description: File operations that do not call normalizePath()

outputs.name: dynamic_test_results
outputs.description: Results of traversal payload testing per service

outputs.name: traversals_escaped_root
outputs.description: Payloads that escaped the service root boundary

assertions.message: All file operations in all services must call normalizePath()
No traversal payload may escape the service root boundary
Static analysis must confirm normalizePath() usage in all file operations
All dynamic traversal tests must pass

challenges.name: SBOM Generation Capability
challenges.description: Verify that the project can generate a Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) in CycloneDX or SPDX format. Check that the Gradle plugin is configured, that the generated SBOM includes all direct and transitive dependencies with version numbers, that license information is present for each dependency, and that the SBOM includes the project's own version metadata.

outputs.name: sbom_generated
outputs.description: Whether SBOM was generated successfully

outputs.name: sbom_format_valid
outputs.description: Whether the SBOM conforms to the specified format schema

outputs.name: total_components
outputs.description: Total number of components listed in the SBOM

outputs.name: components_with_version
outputs.description: Number of components with version information

outputs.name: components_with_license
outputs.description: Number of components with license information

outputs.name: project_metadata_present
outputs.description: Whether the SBOM includes project version metadata

assertions.message: SBOM must be generated successfully
SBOM must conform to the specified format schema (CycloneDX or SPDX)
SBOM must list at least 20 components (direct + transitive dependencies)
SBOM must include project version metadata

challenges.name: CodeQL Configuration and Query Coverage
challenges.description: Verify that CodeQL is configured in the CI pipeline with appropriate query suites for Kotlin/Java analysis. Check that the codeql-config.yml or GitHub Actions workflow includes: (1) 'security-and-quality' query suite, (2) Kotlin and Java language targets, (3) autobuild or custom build step, (4) SARIF upload to GitHub Security tab, (5) scheduled scan frequency (at least weekly), and (6) paths-ignore for generated code and test fixtures.

outputs.name: codeql_configured
outputs.description: Whether CodeQL is configured in CI

outputs.name: query_suite
outputs.description: The CodeQL query suite configured

outputs.name: languages_configured
outputs.description: Programming languages configured for CodeQL analysis

outputs.name: sarif_upload_configured
outputs.description: Whether SARIF results are uploaded to GitHub Security

outputs.name: schedule_configured
outputs.description: Whether scheduled scans are configured

outputs.name: paths_ignore_configured
outputs.description: Whether paths-ignore is set for generated code

assertions.message: CodeQL must be configured in the CI pipeline
CodeQL must use the security-and-quality query suite
CodeQL SARIF results must be uploaded to GitHub Security tab
CodeQL must have scheduled scans configured (at least weekly)
